entries.definition:
yastaḥsirūn once; حَسْرَة ḥasratun seven times; حَسَرَات ḥasarāt
twice; حَسِير ḥasīr once and مَحْسُور maḥsūr once.

يَسْتَحْسِر yastaḥsir [imperf. of v. X اِسْتَحْسَرَ istaḥsara, intrans.] to
tire, to abate, to collapse with fatigue, to become weary (21:19)
وَمَنْ عِندَهُ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِهِ وَلَا يَسْتَحْسِرُونَ and those that are with
Him are never too proud to worship Him, nor do they grow
weary.

حَسْرَة ḥasratun [n.; pl. حَسَرَات ḥasarāt] deep sorrow, anguish
(3:156) لِيَجْعَلَ اللَّهُ ذَٰلِكَ حَسْرَةً فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ that God may make that an
anguish in their hearts * (39:56) يَاحَسْرَتَىٰ woe is me!, woe to me!, O
grief!

حَسِير ḥasīr [quasi-act/pass. part.] weary, defeated (67:3–4) يَنقَلِبْ
إِلَيْكَ الْبَصَرُ خَاسِئًا وَهُوَ حَسِيرٌ your sight will come back to you, weak
and defeated.

مَحْسُور maḥsūr [pass. part.] stripped, bare, destitute, regretful
(17:29) وَلَا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً إِلَىٰ عُنُقِكَ وَلَا تَبْسُطْهَا كُلَّ الْبَسْطِ فَتَقْعُدَ مَلُومًا مَّحْسُورًا
and do not let your hand be chained to your neck, nor outspread
it in complete spreading, lest you end up blamed and
overwhelmed with regret.
entries.definition_english:
yastaḥsirūn once; حَسْرَة ḥasratun seven times; حَسَرَات ḥasarāt
twice; حَسِير ḥasīr once and مَحْسُور maḥsūr once.

يَسْتَحْسِر yastaḥsir [imperf. of v. X اِسْتَحْسَرَ istaḥsara, intrans.] to

حَسْرَة ḥasratun [n.; pl. حَسَرَات ḥasarāt] deep sorrow, anguish

حَسِير ḥasīr [quasi-act/pass. part.] weary, defeated (67:3–4) يَنقَلِبْ

مَحْسُور maḥsūr [pass. part.] stripped, bare, destitute, regretful
(17:29) وَلَا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً إِلَىٰ عُنُقِكَ وَلَا تَبْسُطْهَا كُلَّ الْبَسْطِ فَتَقْعُدَ مَلُومًا مَّحْسُورًا
and do not let your hand be chained to your neck, nor outspread
it in complete spreading, lest you end up blamed and
overwhelmed with regret.

entries.definition:
h–s–s faint noise, hissing sound, whispering sound,
concealed gentle sound; to feel, to perceive through the senses, to
find out, to probe, to spy; to kill, to rout, to strike hard;
movement; to remove the dust. Of this root, four forms occur five
times in the Qurʾan: تَحُسُّونَ taḥussūn once; أَحَسَّ ʾaḥassa three
times; تَحَسَّسُوا taḥassasū once and حَسِيس ḥasīs once.

تَحُسّ taḥuss [imperf. of v. حَسَّ ḥassa, trans.] to slay, to
eradicate, to destroy, to rout (3:152) وَلَقَدْ صَدَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ إِذْ تَحُسُّونَهُم
بِإِذْنِهِ and indeed God fulfilled His promise to you when you were
routing them by His permission.

أَحَسَّ ʾaḥassa [v. IV, trans.] 1 to find, to become conscious of,
to become aware of (3:52) فَلَمَّا أَحَسَّ عِيسَىٰ مِنْهُمُ الْكُفْرَ قَالَ مَنْ أَنصَارِي إِلَى
اللَّهِ when Jesus became aware of their disbelief, he said, ‘Who will
be my helpers in God’s cause?’ 2 to see, to perceive (19:98) وَكَمْ
أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هَلْ تُحِسُّ مِنْهُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ أَوْ تَسْمَعُ لَهُمْ رِكْزًا

209
